assertion_results.text: A base exception class (AppError or similar) is defined with attributes: code, message, status_code, category, and support for __cause__ chaining
assertion_results.evidence: No base exception class. Service uses built-in ValueError and RuntimeError.

assertion_results.text: At least 4 domain-specific exception subclasses exist (e.g., NotFoundError, ValidationError, ServiceUnavailableError, PaymentError)
assertion_results.evidence: Two custom exceptions defined: OrderNotFoundError and PaymentFailedError. Missing ValidationError and ServiceUnavailableError.

assertion_results.text: Exception wrapping uses 'raise NewError(...) from original_error' syntax to preserve the causal chain
assertion_results.evidence: No 'from' syntax used. Errors raised as new exceptions: 'raise RuntimeError(str(e))' which loses the original traceback.

assertion_results.text: No bare 'except:' or 'except Exception:' clauses that swallow errors without re-raising or wrapping
assertion_results.evidence: process_payment has 'except Exception as e: logger.error(f"Payment failed: {e}"); return None' which swallows the exception.

assertion_results.text: Structured logging outputs JSON-formatted log entries with at minimum: code, message, correlation_id, and timestamp
assertion_results.evidence: Uses logging.error(f'Error: {e}') with default text formatter. No JSON format, no correlation_id, no error codes in logs.

assertion_results.text: Database errors (SQLAlchemy exceptions) are caught and wrapped in domain-specific errors with context
assertion_results.evidence: SQLAlchemy errors caught with 'except Exception' and re-raised as generic RuntimeError('Database error') with no domain context.

assertion_results.text: External API errors (httpx/HTTP errors) are caught and wrapped with the upstream service name and response details
assertion_results.evidence: httpx errors caught as generic Exception. Wrapped as RuntimeError('API call failed') with no service name or response details.

assertion_results.text: Each error includes a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_ORDER_NOT_FOUND) not just a status number
assertion_results.evidence: No error codes. Errors identified by exception class name and free-text message only.
